channel.title: Cpp on Pixel Bytes
channel.description: Recent content in Cpp on Pixel Bytes

item.title: Memory Latency Hiding in CUDA using Streams
item.description: I’m starting a new CUDA project to deepen my understanding of GPU acceleration. I’ll begin with simple tasks like vector addition and move on to more involved projects, including image processing and language model optimizations. While this series won’t be a step-by-step tutorial, I’ll share the interesting parts of my implementations, highlighting the challenges I faced and the reasoning behind my decisions. For the complete code, feel free to check out the project repository.

item.title: Reduction, Prefix sum
item.description:
What I will explore in this post

In the sections below, I will compare the PTX and SASS generated for a few variants of the vector addition kernel:

item.title: Reduction, Prefix sum
item.description:
Reduction

CPU Baseline

Recursive Reduction - GPU

Reduction with Interleaved Addressing - Divergent branches

Reduction with Sequential Addressing - Bank Conflicts

  • stall_wait is reduced by 2x, but the overall time is increased by 2x due to bank conflicts. This is because threads in the same warp access different memory addresses that map to the same memory bank, causing serialization of memory accesses and increasing latency.

stall_wait → divergent warp, waiting for divergent threads to finish executing stall_barrier →wait on fixed latency exec dependency (harmless)
